title: HTTPS, TLS Identity, Certificate Trust, Proxies, and Downgrade Boundaries · Browser and Node… · Type-System Trace: Downgrade Mixed Content
summary: A universal browser-and-Node library must state what HTTPS trust it can configure in each runtime and where proxies or mTLS require Node boundaries. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts downgrade mixed content increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch transport trust and certificate evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reject unintended HTTP downgrades, insecure redirects, mixed content, and credential forwarding across weaker channels. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Model forward proxies, CONNECT tunnels, TLS interception, and mutual TLS credentials as separate authority boundaries. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
